rows.definition: internal/vcachecal is the vCache M1 observe-and-calibrate decision layer (#716): the warmth-belief estimator plus the offline probe harness that fits the T/M_min/r warmth law and reports false-warm/false-cold prediction error, off the hot path.
rows.distinction: It only OBSERVES and calibrates cache-warmth belief; it is not vcachewarm, the M3 layer that actually issues warming requests.

rows.definition: internal/vcachescore is the vCache operator scorecard that composes the vcachecal/vcachestar/vcachegov proof leaves into the offline 2x-readiness gate and the hot-anchor index artifact.
rows.distinction: It is a pure off-path readiness gate over the other vCache leaves, not vcachecal which produces the calibration belief it consumes.

rows.definition: internal/vcachewarm is the vCache M3 dedicated-warming decision layer (#718): the Anthropic max_tokens:0-vs-decode-1 gates, byte-identical prefix guard, send-one-then-fan barrier, and wasted-warm accounting.
rows.distinction: It decides how to actively warm an anchor, unlike vcachestar which only decides which first natural request becomes the anchor.

rows.definition: internal/vcachestar is the vCache M2 star-anchor decision layer (#717): canonicalizer-as-gate, wire-byte manifest keying, first-natural-request anchor warming, telemetry demotion, and uncached-first cost booking.
rows.distinction: It chooses and keys the anchor prefix, not vcacheobserve which ingests already-observed provider-cache telemetry after the fact.

rows.definition: internal/vcacheobserve backs `fak vcache observe`, ingesting REAL provider-cache transcripts/telemetry into a per-session and cross-session hit-rate report.
rows.distinction: It is the read-only measurement of realized provider-cache reuse, not vcachestar which is the forward decision layer that places anchors.

rows.definition: internal/cacheobs is the process-global observability tap that the in-kernel KV-prefix cache writes lifecycle events into for scraping.
rows.distinction: It is the in-kernel KV-prefix tap, not cachemeta which is the cross-plane Entry shape the events are normalized into.

rows.definition: CacheHit is a vDSO CacheEventKind value naming a tier-2 cache lifecycle HIT event (a cacheable lookup that an entry served), emitted to the cache-event sink.
rows.distinction: It is the positive lifecycle event, not NOT_CACHED which is the miss reason recorded when no tier entry answered.

rows.definition: MissNotCached ("NOT_CACHED") is the vDSO miss-reason recorded when a lookup was cacheable but no tier-1/tier-3/tier-2 entry answered it.
rows.distinction: It is the miss-classification counter, not CacheHit which records the successful serve.

rows.definition: CachedTokens is the raw provider-reported cache-read token count carried on cachemeta.ProviderCache and on the agent Usage prompt/input token details.
rows.distinction: It is the raw per-response provider counter, not CacheReadMultiplier which is the price coefficient applied to it.

rows.definition: CachedPromptTokens is the agent Usage method (and AdjudicationSummary field) that normalizes Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini prompt-cache hit counts into one cache-read prompt-token number.
rows.distinction: It is the cross-provider-normalized hit count, not CachedTokens which is the un-normalized per-provider raw field it reads from.

rows.definition: healthy_cache is the ResetReasonHealthy value of the resetScore SHADOW policy, the verdict that a turn's OBSERVED provider cache ratios look healthy enough to keep cutting rather than reset.
rows.distinction: It is a recommend-only health verdict over observed ratios, not provider-cache which is the actual external cache being judged.

rows.definition: compute.MemoryKVCache is the MemoryClass label "kv_cache" used by the capacity planner to bucket KV-cache allocations in the device memory plan.
rows.distinction: It is the accounting class label for budgeting bytes, not kv-cache which is the actual K/V tensor store those bytes hold.

rows.definition: model.cachedInvFreq returns the memoized RoPE inverse-frequency table for a config/layer so the rotation base is computed once and reused.
rows.distinction: It is a memoized rotation-table cache for RoPE, not kv-cache which stores per-position key/value tensors.

rows.definition: model.glmDsaKVCache is the per-session KV cache struct for GLM-MoE-DSA sparse attention, holding pre-RoPE K, V, and the index span with reserve/evict/rerotate operations.
rows.distinction: It is the DSA-sparse per-session cache, not the dense full-attention KVCache that NewKVCache allocates.

rows.definition: model.glmDsaAttendCached is the method that computes GLM-MoE-DSA sparse attention over the already-selected keys/values resident in a glmDsaKVCache (softmax(scale·q·k) then ΣwV).
rows.distinction: It is the attend OPERATION over the cache, not glmDsaKVCache which is the storage it reads from.

rows.definition: model.NewKVCache allocates an empty dense full-attention KVCache (keeping pre-RoPE Kraw) for a model session.
rows.distinction: It allocates the dense KV cache, not newLinearAttnCache (recurrent/conv state) nor newGLMDsaKVCache (sparse DSA cache).

rows.definition: model.linearAttnCache (constructed by newLinearAttnCache) is the per-session recurrent/conv state cache for Qwen3.5 hybrid linear-attention layers, held in the KVCache.linear field.
rows.distinction: It holds recurrent linear-attention state, not the per-position key/value tensors of the dense cache that NewKVCache allocates.

rows.definition: model.growCaches grows a slice of *KVCache up to batch size B, allocating fresh per-row caches as needed for batched decode/prefill.
rows.distinction: It scales a batch's worth of KV caches, not NewKVCache which allocates a single empty cache.

rows.definition: compute.uploadCache (and its Metal twin metalUploadCache) shares one device/VRAM copy per distinct host buffer across all sessions so a weight tensor stages to the GPU once.
rows.distinction: It memoizes host-to-device weight uploads keyed by host pointer, not kv-cache which stores per-token attention state.

rows.definition: cachemeta.FromProviderCache lowers a provider-reported ProviderCache observation into a cachemeta.Entry with provider residency and prompt-prefix media type.
rows.distinction: It is the adapter that materializes the Entry, not ProviderCacheVerdict which then judges whether that Entry may be served.

rows.definition: cachemeta.ProviderCacheVerdict is the typed LookupVerdict answering "may this provider cache entry be served", returning cost_latency_only / non-serveable for provider residency.
rows.distinction: It is the serveability judgment, not FromProviderCache which builds the Entry it adjudicates.

rows.definition: agent.rawHasCacheControl is the predicate that tests whether a raw Anthropic JSON block (system, tool_result, or content) carries a cache_control breakpoint.
rows.distinction: It is the detector of a cache_control marker on the wire, not cache-control the concept of the Anthropic prompt-cache breakpoint itself.

rows.definition: gateway.CacheReadMultiplier (=0.1, mirrored in internal/resume) is the price of a cached-prefix READ relative to base input, used to value cache-read tokens in cost math.
rows.distinction: It is the pricing coefficient, not cache-read which is the token count it is multiplied against.

rows.definition: cache_rebate_tokens is the OBSERVED per-turn field in the `fak serve --debug-stats` stderr line, the token-equivalent rebate computed as cacheRead·(1−CacheReadMultiplier).
rows.distinction: It is the realized savings projection printed per turn, not CacheReadMultiplier which is the constant coefficient inside its formula.

rows.definition: gateway.recordCompactionCacheRead records the provider's cache_read_input_tokens on a compacted (streamed or buffered) turn so post-compaction billing truth is captured.
rows.distinction: It is the metrics recorder for cache-read on compacted turns, not cache-read the underlying provider token concept it logs.

rows.definition: PageCacheEntries returns the cachemeta.Entry metadata for every page-table row of a context image (cdb.Image / contextq), without materializing the pages.
rows.distinction: It enumerates per-page cache metadata for an image, not cachemeta which is the single Entry shape each row uses.

rows.definition: vdso.emitCache builds a cachemeta entry from a tier-2 identity and dispatches it (outside the lock) to the installed cache-event sink.
rows.distinction: It is the per-event emitter, not SetCacheEventSink which installs the observer it dispatches to.

rows.definition: vdso.SetCacheEventSink installs (or clears) the observer function that receives tier-2 cache lifecycle CacheEvents, wired by the gateway to fold them into fak_cache_*.
rows.distinction: It is the registration of the observer, not emitCache which fires events into the registered sink.

rows.definition: gateway.vcacheProofFromCounters prices the NET realized provider-cache economics over cumulative input/cached/creation counters into a vcachegov.TelemetrySavingsProof.
rows.distinction: It is the proof-builder over realized counters, not VCacheTokenEquiv the single token-equivalent cost figure that sits inside the proof.

rows.definition: vcachegov.VCacheTokenEquiv is the token-equivalent cost of a vCache-anchored plan (write-multiplier·anchor + reuse reads + suffix), compared against the baseline to compute savings.
rows.distinction: It is the single cost scalar of the anchored plan, not vcacheProofFromCounters which is the surrounding proof that derives and frames it.

rows.definition: The EngineCache* serve flags (engine sglang|vllm, base-url, admin-key env, idle-timeout, require-exact-span) configure fak's reset of a self-hosted upstream serving-engine cache after a quarantined provider-bound tool turn.
rows.distinction: They are the configuration knobs for resetting an external serving engine's cache, not enginecache the in-fak engine-cache subsystem concept they tune.

rows.definition: fak_cache_* is the Prometheus metric family folding cachemeta.Entry events across ALL cache planes (the unified cross-plane events/bytes/breakdown counters rendered by the gateway's StreamMetrics).
rows.distinction: It is the cross-plane unified fold, not fak_engine_cache_* which counts only the live serving-engine's KV cache events.

rows.definition: fak_engine_cache_* is the Prometheus metric family for live serving-engine KV cache events (events/hits/misses/faults/bytes/tokens moved across residency tiers), namespaced alongside fak_gateway_*/fak_kernel_*.
rows.distinction: It counts only the engine's KV cache lifecycle, not fak_cache_* which is the unified fold over every cache plane's cachemeta entries.

rows.definition: cmd/hwcachedemo is a no-model, no-GPU command that proves fak's cache is hardware-aware by replaying an expensive hot-prefix span under hand-injected capacity pressure (demote-not-evict).
rows.distinction: It is the runnable single-stream proof binary, not hardware-aware-cache the cache property it demonstrates.

rows.definition: l3cache is the docs/notes positioning study (epic #79) that fixes fak's relationship to an external, content-addressed remote-DRAM L3 KV-cache tier and maps fak's shipped semantic primitives onto the gaps that tier leaves open.
rows.distinction: It is the external remote-DRAM cache tier fak positions against, not kv-transfer the in-fak primitive for moving KV between local residency tiers.
